<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py-->
{{Expand Japanese|石船神社 (村上市)}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Iwafune Shrine
| native_name = 石船神社
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes
| mapframe-point = none
| map_type = Japan
| map_caption = Iwafune Shrine
| coordinates = {{coord|38.196327|139.432018}}
| map_relief = 1
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
}}

{{nihongo|'''Iwafune Shrine'''|石船神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
| Name = Iwafune Shrine
| Image = {{Image needed|cat=Murakami City}}
| Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Murakami City|Q284503|qid=Q284503|1=Murakami, Niigata}} Iwafune Mikkaichi 9-29
| Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
| Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|Q134862171|qid=Q134862171|1=kuraokaminokami}}
| Sacred object =
| Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-naisha|1=UNKNOWN}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}; head shrine of {{ill|lt=Iwafune District|Q1155124|qid=Q1155124|1=Iwafune District, Niigata}}
| Founded = Before {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|qid=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=648|Q30618|qid=Q30618|1=648}})
| Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
| Alternative name =
| Pilgrimage sites =
| Annual festival = October 18–19
| Rituals = {{ill|lt=Iwafune Grand Festival|Q11475588|qid=Q11475588|1=Iwafune Grand Festival}}
| Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Iwafune Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Iwafune Shrine''' (いわふねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Murakami City|Q284503|qid=Q284503|1=Murakami, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shikinai shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, and is the foremost of the eight shrines of {{ill|lt=Iwafune District|Q1155124|qid=Q1155124|lt=Iwafune District|1=Iwafune District, Niigata}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/102 |title=Engishiki Vol. 2 |access-date=2024-5-6}}</ref><ref name="tourism">[https://www.sake3.com/spot/858 Iwafune Shrine] Murakami City Tourism Association “Murakami City Tourism Information Dispatch Base”</ref><ref name="walk">Editorial Committee of *Historical Walks of Niigata Prefecture*, ed., *Historical Walks of Niigata Prefecture (Historical Walks 15)*, Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2009, pp. 214–215</ref>.

Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, it was ranked as the first {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Niigata Prefecture<ref name="tourism" />.

=== History ===
The shrine is enshrined on the southern foothills of Myojin-yama.

It is said that the shrine already existed at the time the {{ill|lt=Iwanofune stockade|Q11587922|qid=Q11587922|1=Iwafuke Palisade}} was established, prior to {{ill|lt=648|Q30618|qid=Q30618|1=648}} (Taika 4), meaning it must have been founded even earlier. Although the location of the Iwanofune stockade remains unknown, there is a theory that it was located near this shrine. Archaeological investigations conducted in 1957–1958 uncovered stone-paved structural remains and dwelling sites, and this area has been tentatively identified as the site of the Iwanofune stockade<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://engishiki.org/echigo/bun/ecg360701-01.html |title=Iwafune Shrine |access-date=2024-5-6}}</ref>. The *Echigo Nayori* records that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} “arrived riding the stone ship, the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|qid=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}}”<ref>From *Studies on Shikinai Shrines*, Vol. 8</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=807|Q30016|qid=Q30016|1=807}} ({{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 2), {{ill|lt=Akishino Yasuhito|Q11595792|qid=Q11595792|1=Akishino no Yasuhito}}, an inspector of the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|qid=Q3139239|1=Hokurikudō}} region, constructed the shrine buildings<ref name="tourism"></ref>. At that time, a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Kifune Shrine|Q276779|qid=Q276779|1=Kifune Shrine}} (Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City) was {{ill|lt=invited|Q11412524|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} and {{ill|lt=enshrined together|Q11412524|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}.

In {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4 (1714), the shrine was granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}.

Within the shrine, a sacred boat called “Myojin-maru (Ofune-sama)” is enshrined, said to be the vessel used when the deity descended from the divine realm to the human world. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Yataza Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|qid=Q11584205|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, which is regarded as a deity of air transportation, this shrine is believed to have a strong association with maritime transportation.

=== Enshrined deities ===
Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto; {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami}}; {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami}}.

=== Auxiliary shrines ===
* Wakayama Shrine and Konpira Shrine  
* Myojin Gongen Shrine  
* Ōyamatsumi Shrine  
* Furu Mine Shrine  
* Iwafune Shōkon Shrine, among others

=== Cultural properties ===
* Iwafune Shrine Sacred Forest (designated Natural Monument of Niigata Prefecture, designated March 5, 1958)<ref name="pref">[https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/life/602399_1719772_misc.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties] Niigata Prefecture / Japan</ref>
* Shagiri float procession and the “Tomoyama” ritual of the Iwafune Festival<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/site/kanko/iwafune-taisai.html |title=Iwafune Grand Festival |access-date=2024-5-7}}</ref> (designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Niigata Prefecture on March 25, 1988)<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/life/602399_1719772_misc.pdf |title=National and Niigata Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2024-5-7}}</ref>

=== Access ===
* 40-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Iwafune-machi Station|Q6100285|qid=Q6100285|1=Iwafunemachi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=JR|Q864495|qid=Q864495|1=Japan Railways Group}} {{ill|lt=Uetsu Main Line|Q1056211|qid=Q1056211|1=Uetsu Main Line}}
* 20 minutes by bus bound for Iwafune or Shioya from {{ill|lt=Murakami Station (Niigata)|Q6937257|qid=Q6937257|lt=Murakami Station|1=Murakami Station (Niigata)}} on the JR Uetsu Main Line; get off at “Iwafune Shrine-mae” bus stop, immediately nearby

=== See also ===
* {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai Shrines in Echigo Province|Q11636502|qid=Q11636502|1=List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province}} 
* {{ill|lt=Iwafune Shrine (Katano City)|Q11587926|qid=Q11587926|1=Iwafune Shrine}}
